item.title: Resources
item.description:
As a developer and researcher, I rely on a variety of tools to streamline my workflow, enhance my productivity. This page is a curated collection of my essential resources. I hope you find them as valuable as I do!

These resources are essential to my workflow, enabling me to learn, research, code, and collaborate effectively.

I hope this collection provides you with valuable tools and inspiration for your own journey. I’d love to hear about your favorite resources as well – feel free to share your recommendations!

item.title: Personal Notes on Public Key Authentication
item.description:
Introduction

SSH (Secure Shell) in a network protocol used for communication securely between two devices. it is primarily used to connect to remote machines, such as servers and workstations, from various client devices, including laptops, and mobile phones. For the authentication SSH offers several methods: passwords (which are less secure), public key authentication (more secure), and other less common techniques like two-factor authentication.

To use SSH protocol with public key authentication, you typically follow three stages: 1.Generate a key-pair 2.Install the public key on the server 3.Connect without a password

item.title: SSH Port Forwarding: My Practical Notes
item.description:
Personal notes from setting up SSH port forwarding.

Local Port Forwarding

This allows you to access a remote service as if it were running locally. Use Case: Accessing a database or service on a remote server that is not exposed to the public internet.

On the server

Start a service (example using a Python web server):

python -m http.server 1234

On my laptop

Forward local port 8080 to remote port 1234:
